description:
You are a storage administrator who is tired of typing the same long disk-space commands every day.  
    Create a tiny build system with a Makefile that automates three related chores.

    1. Folder layout (all paths are absolute):
       • /home/user/disk_admin/Makefile              ← the file you must author  
       • /home/user/disk_admin/reports/              ← already exists; will hold log files  
       • /home/user/disk_admin/reports/archives/     ← must be created by the Makefile when needed  

    2. Pre-existing material: two text files already sit inside /home/user/disk_admin/reports/  
       • old_app1.log  
       • old_app2.log  
       Do NOT rename these; the automated grader will look for them.

    3. Required Makefile targets (names are case-sensitive):

       a) report  
          • When the target runs it must produce exactly one file:
            /home/user/disk_admin/reports/latest_report.log
          • latest_report.log must follow the precise five-section layout below
            (the grader checks the literal section headers and blank-line placement):

            Line 1: Report generated on YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS  
            Line 2:  
            Line 3: === Filesystem Summary ===  
            (raw, multi-line output of df -h comes here)  
            (blank line)  
            === Home Usage ===  
            (raw, multi-line output of du -sh /home/user/* | sort -h comes here)  

            Notes:  
            – The first line’s timestamp must be generated at run time.  
            – There must be exactly one completely blank line after the df -h block.  
            – Both “=== Filesystem Summary ===” and “=== Home Usage ===” headers are mandatory.  

       b) archive  
          • Compress every *.log file that is present inside
            /home/user/disk_admin/reports/ **except** latest_report.log.  
          • The compressed file must be named disk_reports_archive.tar.gz and placed in
            /home/user/disk_admin/reports/archives/  
          • After the archive is created, the original log files that went into the archive
            must be removed (leaving only latest_report.log uncompressed).  
          • Re-running the target when there are no *.log files to archive should be a
            no-op that exits cleanly.

       c) clean  
          • Delete   /home/user/disk_admin/reports/archives/disk_reports_archive.tar.gz  
          • Do not delete the archives directory itself or any *.log files.

       d) all (optional quality-of-life target)  
          • all should simply depend on report, then archive.

    4. Behavioural expectations that the grader will verify:
       • /home/user/disk_admin/Makefile exists and contains the three target names
         “report:”, “archive:”, and “clean:”.  
       • After running “make -C /home/user/disk_admin report” the file
         /home/user/disk_admin/reports/latest_report.log exists and  
         • its very first line starts with the literal text “Report generated on ”  
         • somewhere in the file appears the header “=== Filesystem Summary ===”  
         • somewhere in the file appears the header “=== Home Usage ===”  
       • After subsequently running “make -C /home/user/disk_admin archive”  
         • /home/user/disk_admin/reports/archives/disk_reports_archive.tar.gz exists  
         • neither /home/user/disk_admin/reports/old_app1.log nor
           /home/user/disk_admin/reports/old_app2.log exist any more  
         • /home/user/disk_admin/reports/latest_report.log still exists untouched.  
       • After running “make -C /home/user/disk_admin clean” the tar.gz file no longer
         exists, but the archives directory and latest_report.log are still present.

    5. Important implementation notes:
       • You must not hard-code absolute timestamps or disk sizes; call the real commands.  
       • Use shell recipe lines that work under /bin/sh (no bash-only features).  
       • Do not require root privileges; everything happens under /home/user.  
       • The Makefile must be idempotent: running the same target twice should not raise
         errors or duplicate work.  

    6. Once your Makefile works, test it manually in the terminal exactly in this order:
       a) make -C /home/user/disk_admin report  
       b) make -C /home/user/disk_admin archive  
       c) make -C /home/user/disk_admin clean  

    No additional commentary is necessary—only ensure that the files and
    directories described above are in the correct state.
truth:
<!--
    Ground-truth information for automated grading.
    DO NOT reveal anything inside this block in the public task description
    ------------------------------------------------------------------------ -->
    Pre-existing files before the agent starts:
      /home/user/disk_admin/reports/old_app1.log      (contains the single line "dummy1")
      /home/user/disk_admin/reports/old_app2.log      (contains the single line "dummy2")

    Files that must exist at grading time:

      1. /home/user/disk_admin/Makefile
         • Must include the literal target labels: "report:", "archive:", "clean:" (with colons).
         • Must include the literal string "df -h".
         • Must include the literal string "du -sh".

      2. /home/user/disk_admin/reports/latest_report.log
         • First line regex: ^Report generated on [0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2} [0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}$
         • Must contain the exact header line "=== Filesystem Summary ==="
         • Must contain the exact header line "=== Home Usage ==="

      3. /home/user/disk_admin/reports/archives/disk_reports_archive.tar.gz
         • File must exist after "make archive" and before "make clean".
         • The tarball must contain two members named old_app1.log and old_app2.log.

    Files that must NOT exist after "make archive" (and still absent at grading):
      /home/user/disk_admin/reports/old_app1.log
      /home/user/disk_admin/reports/old_app2.log

    Files that must NOT exist after "make clean":
      /home/user/disk_admin/reports/archives/disk_reports_archive.tar.gz
